speaker: Guest
text_en: Every country in the Middle East, except maybe the UAE, is desperate to avoid this war. The Chinese and the Russians don't want this war. Trump really doesn't want this war. Who wants this war? Benjamin Netanyahu.
text: 中东每个国家——也许除了 UAE——都在拼命避免这场战争。中国人和俄罗斯人不想打这场仗。Trump 也真的不想打这场仗。那到底是谁想打？Benjamin Netanyahu。

speaker: Host
text_en: Will he get the war that he wants?
text: 那他会打上他想要的这场战争吗？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Boy, it's very hard to say. I go back and forth on this one. On one hand, I say this war can't happen because we can't achieve our goals by striking Iran.
text: 哎，这个很难说。我在这个问题上一直摇摆不定。一方面，我觉得这场战争不可能发生，因为光靠打击 Iran，我们根本实现不了目标。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And furthermore, you have to take into account what Iran can do in retaliation. You were just talking about the Straits of Hormuz and what the consequences would be if that one heated up.
text: 而且，你还得把 Iran 的报复能力算进去。你刚才就在说 Hormuz 海峡，如果那边局势升温，会带来什么后果。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And of course, the Iranians are going to launch ballistic missiles at Israel as well. So it's hard to see why it makes sense to start this war from a purely strategic point of view. But then it looks like Trump has boxed himself in.
text: 当然，Iran 也会向 Israel 发射弹道导弹。所以，单从战略角度看，很难理解为什么发动这场战争是合理的。但另一方面，看起来 Trump 已经把自己逼进死角了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And furthermore, the Israelis appear to be putting enormous pressure on him to go to war against Iran. And, you know, can he disobey his masters? I don't think so. So that pushes me in the other direction.
text: 而且，Israel 人显然正在对他施加巨大的压力，要他对 Iran 开战。再说了，你知道的，他能违抗自己的主子吗？我看不能。所以这又把我推向了另一种判断。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So I feel one day war is inevitable because of pressure from the Israelis. And the next day, I think it's not going to happen because I can't tell the story as to how a war leads to some sort of happy ending for all the parties.
text: 所以我有时候觉得，在 Israel 的压力之下，战争不可避免；可到了第二天，我又觉得不会发生，因为我实在讲不出一个故事，说明这场战争最后怎么会给各方带来某种皆大欢喜的结局。
